title: pedilanthus tithymaloides subsp. angustifolius
intro: Pedilanthus tithymaloides subsp. angustifolius is a subspecies of the Pedilanthus tithymaloides plant, commonly known as the 'Devil's Backbone' due to its unique, branching habit resembling a thorny spine. This succulent plant is native to Central and South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Pedilanthus tithymaloides subsp. angustifolius include relief from fever, pain, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and crushing the leaves, which can then be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use.
